Name: Spinarak
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: It spins a web using fine yet durable thread. It then waits patiently for prey to be trapped.

Name: Ariados
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During your opponent’s next turn, that Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: It spins string not only from its rear but also from its mouth. It’s hard to tell which end is which.

Name: Shaymin
Effects: Search your deck for an Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Benched [G] Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It can dissolve toxins in the air to instantly transform ruined land into a lush field of flowers.

Name: Snivy
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It prefers to avoid groups. In its day-to-day life, it dexterously controls its vines to compensate for its short arms.

Name: Servine
Flavor-Text: Servine’s one weakness is its inflated sense of pride. It takes a while for Servine to be able to cooperate with other Pokémon.

Name: Serperior
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Pokémon in play.
If Rosa’s Encouragement is in your discard pile, this attack does 150 more damage.
Flavor-Text: An intimidating gaze and majestic appearance have caused this Pokémon to be known as the Lord of the Forest.

Name: Scatterbug
Flavor-Text: The powder that covers its body regulates its temperature, so it can live in any region or climate.

Name: Spewpa
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It lives hidden within thicket shadows. When predators attack, it bristles the fur covering its body in an effort to threaten them.

Name: Vivillon
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Your opponent shuffles their hand and puts it on the bottom of their deck. If they put any cards on the bottom of their deck in this way, they draw 4 cards.
Effects: If a Stadium is in play, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The patterns on this Pokémon depend on the climate and topography of the land it was born in. This form is from lands where flowers bloom.

Name: Rowlet
Effects: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It feels relaxed in tight, dark places and has been known to nestle into its Trainer’s bag or the front of their jacket.

Name: Dartrix
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon, and this attack does 90 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It throws sharp feathers called blade quills at enemies or prey. It seldom misses.

Name: Decidueye ex
Ability-Effect: If your opponent has exactly 4 cards in their hand, ignore all [C] Energy in the costs of attacks used by this Pokémon.
Effects: Discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Fletchinder
Flavor-Text: The hotter the flame sac in its belly, the faster it can fly, but it takes some time to get the fire going.

Name: Talonflame
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Flip a coin. If heads, discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: In the fever of an exciting battle, it showers embers from the gaps between its feathers as it flies.

Name: Salandit
Flavor-Text: Only female Salandit can produce gas laden with pheromones. Males entranced by this gas will do whatever the females tell them.

Name: Salazzle ex
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned and Poisoned. Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Turtonator
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), discard an Energy from the Attacking Pokémon.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Explosive substances coat the shell on its back. Enemies that dare attack it will be blown away by an immense detonation.

Name: Seel
Flavor-Text: The colder it gets, the better it feels. It joyfully swims around oceans so cold that they are filled with floating ice.

Name: Dewgong
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may use this Ability. Move a [W] Energy from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It stores thermal energy in its body to fend off the cold. It swims at eight knots even in frigid waters.

Name: Staryu
Flavor-Text: Even if its body is torn, it can regenerate as long as the glowing central core remains intact.

Name: Mega Starmie ex
Effects: This attack also does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance, or by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Lapras ex
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon. Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Amaura
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: This ancient Pokémon was restored from a part of its body that had been frozen in ice for 100 million years.

Name: Aurorus
Ability-Effect: All of your Pokémon that have any [W] Energy attached take 50 less damage from attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance). The effect of Tundra Wall doesn’t stack.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t use attacks.
Flavor-Text: The diamond-shaped crystals on its body expel air as cold as -240 degrees Fahrenheit, surrounding its enemies and encasing them in ice.

Name: Volcanion
Effects: Flip a coin for each [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 90 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It lets out billows of steam and disappears into the dense fog. It’s said to live in mountains where humans do not tread.

Name: Shinx
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: The extension and contraction of its muscles generates electricity. Its fur glows when it’s in trouble.

Name: Luxio
Ability-Effect: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this Pokémon can evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.
Flavor-Text: By gathering their tails together, they collectively generate powerful electricity from their claws.

Name: Luxray
Effects: This attack does 70 damage for each Prize card you have taken.
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Luxray’s ability to see through objects comes in handy when it’s scouting for danger.

Name: Dedenne
Effects: Choose Basic [L] Energy cards from your discard pile up to the amount of Energy attached to all of your opponent’s Pokémon and attach them to your [L] Pokémon in any way you like.
Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Its whiskers serve as antennas. By sending and receiving electrical waves, it can communicate with others over vast distances.

Name: Clefairy
Effects: Switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.
Flavor-Text: The moonlight that it stores in the wings on its back apparently gives it the ability to float in midair.

Name: Mega Clefable ex
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of your opponent’s Pokémon’s Abilities done to this Pokémon.
Effects: You may discard up to 4 Energy cards from your hand, and this attack does 40 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Mawile
Effects: Discard up to 2 Energy cards from your hand, and this attack does 60 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: Attached to its head is a huge set of jaws formed by horns. It can chew through iron beams.

Name: Espurr
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It keeps its intense psychic power from leaking out by using its ears to cover the organs emitting that power.

Name: Meowstic
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Females are a bit more selfish and aggressive than males. If they don’t get what they want, they will torment you with their psychic abilities.

Name: Spritzee
Effects: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: In the past, rather than using perfume, royal ladies carried a Spritzee that would waft a fragrance they liked.

Name: Aromatisse
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Search your deck for up to 2 Basic [P] Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its scent is so overpowering that, unless they happen to really enjoy the smell, Trainers will have a hard time walking alongside it.

Name: Nosepass
Flavor-Text: When endangered, it may protect itself by increasing its magnetism and drawing nearby iron objects to its body.

Name: Probopass
Effects: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Probopass radiates such a powerful magnetic field that nearby electrical appliances are rendered useless while it’s around.

Name: Hippopotas
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to use an attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn’t happen.
Flavor-Text: It enshrouds itself with sand to protect itself from germs. It does not enjoy getting wet.

Name: Hippowdon
Effects: If you played Tarragon from your hand during this turn, discard the top 3 cards of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: It blasts internally stored sand from ports on its body to create a towering twister for attack.

Name: Landorus
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Put an Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.
Flavor-Text: Lands visited by Landorus grant such bountiful crops that it has been hailed as “The Guardian of the Fields.”

Name: Binacle
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Flavor-Text: Two Binacle live together on one rock. When they fight, one of them will move to a different rock.

Name: Barbaracle
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Attach a Basic [F] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your [F] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Barbaracle’s legs and hands have minds of their own, and they will move independently. But they usually follow the head’s orders.

Name: Tyrunt
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon was restored from a fossil. If something happens that it doesn’t like, it throws a tantrum and runs wild.

Name: Tyrantrum
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached, it gets +150 HP.
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon behaved like a king 100 million years ago. Nothing could stop it.

Name: Hawlucha
Effects: If your Benched Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: With its wings, it controls its position in the air. It likes to attack from above, a maneuver that is difficult to defend against.

Name: Mega Zygarde ex
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
For each of your opponent’s Pokémon, flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 150 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Gastly
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It’s born from gases. Anyone would faint if engulfed by its gaseous body, which contains poison.

Name: Haunter
Effects: Place 3 damage counters on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It licks with its gaseous tongue to steal its victim’s life force. It lurks in darkness, waiting for prey.

Name: Gengar
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon, put it into your hand instead of the discard pile. (Discard all attached cards.)
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It hides in shadows. It is said that in rooms where Gengar is hiding, the temperature drops by nearly 10 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Skorupi
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: After burrowing into the sand, it waits patiently for prey to come near. This Pokémon and Sizzlipede share common descent.

Name: Drapion
Effects: This Pokémon also does 70 damage to itself. Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Its poison is potent, but it rarely sees use. This Pokémon prefers to use physical force instead, going on rampages with its car-crushing strength.

Name: Yveltal ex
Effects: Knock Out each of your opponent’s Pokémon that has 50 HP or less remaining.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Dark Strike.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Chien-Pao
Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The hatred of those who perished by the sword long ago has clad itself in snow and become a Pokémon.

Name: Mega Skarmory ex
Effects: Shuffle all Energy attached to this Pokémon into your deck, and this attack does 220 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Honedge
Flavor-Text: Apparently Honedge was born when a departed spirit began inhabiting an ancient sword. It attaches itself to people and drinks their life force.

Name: Doublade
Effects: Reveal any number of Honedge, Doublade, and Aegislash from your hand, and this attack does 60 damage for each card you revealed in this way.
Flavor-Text: When Honedge evolves, it divides into two swords that cooperate via telepathy to coordinate attacks and slash their enemies to ribbons.

Name: Aegislash
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use attacks.
Flavor-Text: This stance is dedicated to offense. It can cleave any oppenent with the strength and weight of its steel blade.

Name: Klefki
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s attacks. During your opponent’s next turn, that Pokémon can’t use that attack.
Flavor-Text: It never lets go of a key that it likes, so people give it the keys to vaults and safes as a way to prevent crime.

Name: Rattata
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Its incisors grow continuously throughout its life. If its incisors get too long, this Pokémon becomes unable to eat, and it starves to death.

Name: Raticate
Effects: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
This attack does 40 damage for each damage counter on all of your Benched Rattata.
Flavor-Text: People say that it fled from its enemies by using its small webbed hind feet to swim from island to island in Alola.

Name: Meowth ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may use this Ability. Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. You can’t use more than 1 Ability that has “Last-Ditch” in its name each turn.
Effects: Put this Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Snorlax
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. Search your deck for an amount of Basic Energy up to the number of heads and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
This Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Its stomach’s digestive juices can dissolve any kind of poison. Eating things off the ground doesn’t bother it at all.

Name: Bunnelby
Flavor-Text: They use their large ears to dig burrows. They will dig the whole night through without rest.

Name: Diggersby
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: With their powerful ears, they can heft boulders of a ton or more with ease. They can be a big help at construction sites.

Name: Fletchling
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon with [F] Resistance, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Despite the beauty of its lilting voice, it’s merciless to intruders that enter its territory.

Name: Furfrou
Effects: Discard random cards from your opponent’s hand until they have 5 cards in their hand.
Flavor-Text: Historically, in the Kalos region, these Pokémon were the designated guardians of the king.

Name: Antique Jaw Fossil
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, attacks used by your opponent’s Active Pokémon do 30 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Description: Play this card as if it were a 60-HP Basic [C] Pokémon. This card can’t be affected by any Special Conditions and can’t retreat.At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Antique Sail Fossil
Ability-Effect: Whenever your opponent plays a Supporter card from their hand, prevent all effects of that card done to this Pokémon.
Description: Play this card as if it were a 60-HP Basic [C] Pokémon. This card can’t be affected by any Special Conditions and can’t retreat.At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Core Memory
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Description: The Mega Zygarde ex this card is attached to can use the attack on this card. (You still need the necessary Energy to use this attack.)
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Crushing Hammer
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Energy Search
Description: Search your deck for a Basic Energy card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Energy Swatter
Description: Your opponent reveals their hand, and you choose an Energy card you find there and put it on the bottom of their deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Hole-Digging Shovel
Description: Discard the top 2 cards of your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Jacinthe
Description: Heal 150 damage from 1 of your [P] Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Judge
Description: Each player shuffles their hand into their deck and draws 4 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Lumiose City
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may search their deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto their Bench. Then, that player shuffles their deck. If a player searches their deck in this way, their turn ends.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Lumiose Galette
Description: Heal 20 damage and remove a Special Condition from your Active Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Naveen
Description: Draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand. Before drawing cards, you may discard any number of cards from your hand. (If you can’t draw any cards in this way, you can’t use this card.)
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Poké Ball
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Poké Pad
Description: Search your deck for a Pokémon that doesn’t have a Rule Box, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Pokémon Catcher
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Potion
Description: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Rosa’s Encouragement
Description: You can use this card only if you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent.Attach up to 2 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Stage 2 Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Tarragon
Description: Put up to 4 in any combination of [F] Pokémon and Basic [F] Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Growing [G] Energy
Description:
As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides [G] Energy.
The [G] Pokémon this card is attached to gets +20 HP.

Name: Rocky [F] Energy
Description:
As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides [F] Energy.
Prevent all effects of attacks used by your opponent’s Pokémon done to the [F] Pokémon this card is attached to. (Existing effects are not removed. Damage is not an effect.)

Name: Telepathic [P] Energy
Description:
As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides [P] Energy.
When you attach this card from your hand to a [P] Pokémon, search your deck for up to 2 Basic [P] Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It lives hidden within thicket shadows. When predators attack, it bristles the fur covering its body in an effort to threaten them.

Effects: Put this Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Energy Recycler
Description: Shuffle up to 5 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Forest of Vitality
Description: Each player’s [G] Pokémon can evolve into [G] Pokémon during the turn they play those Pokémon, except during their first turn.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Rosa’s Encouragement
Description: You can use this card only if you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent.Attach up to 2 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Stage 2 Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Sacred Ash
Description: Shuffle up to 5 Pokémon from your discard pile into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Tarragon
Description: Put up to 4 in any combination of [F] Pokémon and Basic [F] Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Wondrous Patch
Description: Attach a Basic [P] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched [P] Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Mega Starmie ex
Effects: This attack also does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance, or by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Mega Clefable ex
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of your opponent’s Pokémon’s Abilities done to this Pokémon.
Effects: You may discard up to 4 Energy cards from your hand, and this attack does 40 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Mega Zygarde ex
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
For each of your opponent’s Pokémon, flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 150 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Meowth ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may use this Ability. Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. You can’t use more than 1 Ability that has “Last-Ditch” in its name each turn.
Effects: Put this Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Jacinthe
Description: Heal 150 damage from 1 of your [P] Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Rosa’s Encouragement
Description: You can use this card only if you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent.Attach up to 2 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Stage 2 Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Mega Zygarde ex
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
For each of your opponent’s Pokémon, flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 150 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Mega-ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.
